exports.name: createGame
exports.signature: (config: GameConfig, displayName: string) -> Promise<{gameId: string, shareCode: string, joinUrl: string}>; creates the Firestore game document with the creator as the first participant and returns the share code + a fully-qualified join URL anchored at the current location.origin + location.pathname plus ?join=<code>

exports.name: joinGame
exports.signature: (shareCode: string, displayName: string) -> Promise<{gameId: string} | {error: 'not_found' | 'game_full' | 'permission_denied'}>; appends the current UID to the game's participants array (subject to Security Rules); returns an error tagged with the failure mode

invariants: joinUrl is computed at createGame time using window.location, so it correctly reflects whichever GitHub Pages URL the artifact is published under.
joinGame must surface 'game_full' distinctly so section-5 can render an appropriate error message rather than a generic 'permission denied'.
